steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The nitrogen-treated SiC fiber has a lower minimum creep rate than the two polymer-derived fibers. A lower creep rate means the material deforms under stress more slowly, indicating superior resistance.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — polymer-derived fibers are stronger overall: This contradicts the data showing the nitrogen-treated fiber performs better. ✗
  • Choice B — composites with nitrogen-treated fiber withstand stress longer: Lower creep rate directly implies the material resists deformation longer under the same stress. ✓
  • Choice C — only under certain temperature conditions: No temperature condition is stated in the comparison. ✗
  • Choice D — polymer-derived fibers are more cost-effective: Cost is not discussed anywhere in the passage. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
